title: Round whole numbers word problems
description: Solve word problems by rounding whole numbers.
topic_path: Module 1: Place value, rounding, and algorithms for addition and subtraction
Topic C: Rounding multi-digit whole numbers

assessment_items.question_content:
A trumpet player spends  $315$ minutes in one month practicing his instrument. 

**Round the practice time to the nearest ten.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] minutes
hints: We want to round to the tens place, so we need to look at the digit in the one place.

The number of minutes was $31\blueD5$.  

The digit in the ones place is $\blueD5$.

Because the digit in the ones place is $5$ or greater, we round the tens place **up** to the nearest ten.

Although $315$ is in the middle of  $310$ and $320$, rounding rules tell us to round **up**.

[[☃ image 1]]
The number of minutes rounded to the nearest ten is $320$.

assessment_items.question_content:
At a barbecue to celebrate the end of the soccer season, $1{,}354$ hot dogs were served. 

**Round the number of hot dogs to the nearest ten.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] hotdogs
hints: We want to round to the tens place, so we need to look at the digit in the one place.

The number of hot dogs was $1{,}35\blueD4$.  

The digit in the ones place is $\blueD4$.

Because the digit in the ones place is less than $5$, we round the tens place **down ** to the nearest ten.

$1{,}354$ is closer to $1{,}350$ than $1{,}360$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The number of hot dogs rounded to the nearest ten is $1{,}350$.

assessment_items.question_content:
On Tuesday, the school cafeteria served $973$ lunches. 

**Round the number of lunches to the nearest hundred.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] lunches
hints: We want to round to the hundreds place, so we need to look at the digit in the tens place.

The number of lunches was $9\blueD73$.  

The digit in the tens place is $\blueD7$.

Because the digit in the tens place is $5$ or greater, we round the hundreds place **up** to the nearest hundred.

$973$ is closer to $1{,}000$ than $900$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The number of lunches rounded to the nearest hundred is $1{,}000$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The attendance at a local basketball game was $8{,}643$ people.

**Round the attendance to the nearest hundred.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] people
hints: We want to round to the hundreds place, so we need to look at the digit in the tens place.

The attendance was $8{,}6\blueD43$ people.

The digit in the tens place is $\blueD4$.

Because the digit in the tens place is less than $5$, we round the hundreds place **down** to the nearest hundred.

$8{,}643$ is closer to $8{,}600$ than $8{,}700$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The attendance rounded to the nearest hundred is $8{,}600$ people.

assessment_items.question_content:
Dax used $6{,}428$ blocks to build a castle.

**Round the number of blocks to the nearest hundred.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] blocks
hints: We want to round to the hundreds place, so we need to look at the digit in the tens place.

Dax used $6{,}4\blueD28$ blocks.

The digit in the tens place is $\blueD2$.

Because the digit in the tens place is less than $5$, we round the hundreds place **down** to the nearest hundred.

$6{,}428$ is closer to $6{,}400$ than $6{,}500$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The number of blocks rounded to the nearest hundred is $6{,}400$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A recycling truck picks up  $4{,}535$ kilograms of cardboard from a grocery store.

**Round the weight of the cardboard to the nearest hundred.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kilograms
hints: We want to round to the hundreds place, so we need to look at the digit in the tens place.

The cardboard weighs  $4{,}5\blueD35$ kilograms.

The digit in the tens place is $\blueD3$.

Because the digit in the tens place is less than $5$, we round the hundreds place **down** to the nearest hundred.

$4{,}535$ is closer to $4{,}500$ than $4{,}600$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The weight of the cardboard rounded to the nearest hundred is $4{,}500$ kilograms.

assessment_items.question_content:
The city of Joyville has $87{,}388$ residents.

**Round the number of people who live in Joyville to the nearest thousand.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] residents
hints: We want to round to the thousands place, so we need to look at the digit in the hundreds place.

Joyville has $87{,}\blueD388$  residents.

The digit in the hundreds place is $\blueD3$.

Because the digit in the hundreds place is less than $5$, we round the thousands place **down** to the nearest thousand.

$87{,}388$ is closer to $87{,}000$ than $88{,}000$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The number of people who live in Joyville rounded to the nearest thousand is $87{,}000$.

assessment_items.question_content:
A school bus weighs  $15{,}591$ kilograms.

**Round the weight of the bus to the nearest thousand.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kilograms
hints: We want to round to the thousands place, so we need to look at the digit in the hundreds place.

The bus weighs $15{,}\blueD591$ kilograms.

The digit in the hundreds place is $\blueD5$.

Because the digit in the hundreds place is $5$ or greater, we round the thousands place **up** to the nearest thousand.

$15{,}591$ is closer to $16{,}000$ than $15{,}000$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The weight of the bus rounded to the nearest thousand is $16{,}000$ kilograms.

assessment_items.question_content:
The distance to the moon is an average of $384{,}400$ kilometers.

**Round the distance  to the nearest ten thousand.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] kilometers
hints: We want to round to the ten thousands place, so we need to look at the digit in the thousands place.

The distance to the moon is  $38\blueD{4}{,}400$  kilometers.

The digit in the thousands place is $\blueD{4}$.

Because the digit in the thousands place is less than $5$, we round the ten  thousands place **down** to the nearest  ten thousand.

$384{,}400$ is closer to $380{,}000$ than $390{,}000$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The distance  to the moon rounded to the nearest ten thousand is $380{,}000$ kilometers.

assessment_items.question_content:
The population of a certain country is $672{,}905$ people.

**Round the population to the nearest ten thousand.**

[[☃ numeric-input 1]] people
hints: We want to round to the ten thousands place, so we need to look at the digit in the thousands place.

The population is  $67\blueD2{,}905$  people.

The digit in the thousands place is $\blueD2$.

Because the digit in the thousands place is less than $5$, we round the ten  thousands place **down** to the nearest  ten thousand.

$672{,}905$ is closer to $670{,}000$ than $680{,}000$.

[[☃ image 1]]
The population rounded to the nearest ten thousand is $670{,}000$ people.

assessment_items.question_content:
A grocery store rounded the number of pounds of bananas they sold last week to $400$ kilograms.

**Which could have been the number of kilograms of bananas sold if the weight was rounded to the nearest hundred?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $400$ is $350$.

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $400$ is $449$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $350$ through $449$ rounds to $400$.
The number of kilograms of bananas sold by the store last week could have been $357$ or $425$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The architect of a new skyscraper rounds the height of the building to $320$ meters. 

**How many meters tall could the building be if the height was rounded to the nearest ten?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $320$ is $315$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $320$ is $324$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $315$ through $324$ rounds to $320$.
The building could be  $316$ meters or $324$ meters tall.

assessment_items.question_content:
The display at an aquarium rounds the weight of a beluga whale  to $1{,}500$ kilograms.  

**How many kilograms could the whale weigh if the weight was rounded to the nearest hundred?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $1{,}500$ is $1{,}450$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $1{,}500$ is $1{,}549$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $1{,}450$ through $1{,}549$ rounds to $1{,}500$.
The weight of the whale could be $1{,}462$ or $1{,}540$ kilograms.

assessment_items.question_content:
The Grayson family got a new backyard swimming pool. When they tell their friends how much water it holds, they round the number to $51{,}100$ liters. 

**Which could be the amount of water in the pool if they rounded the number of liters to the nearest hundred?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $51{,}100$ is $51{,}050$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $51{,}100$ is $51{,}149$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $51{,}050$ through $51{,}149$ rounds to $51{,}100$.
The number of liters of water in the pool  could be $51{,}080$ or $51{,}122$.

assessment_items.question_content:
The distance traveled by a ship crossing the Pacific Ocean is rounded to  $15{,}500$ kilometers. 

**Which could be the number of kilometers the ship traveled if the distance was rounded to the nearest hundred?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $15{,}500$ is $15{,}450$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $15{,}500$ is $15{,}549$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $15{,}450$ through $15{,}549$ rounds to $15{,}500$.
The number of kilometers the ship traveled could have been $15{,}490$  and $15{,}538$ .

assessment_items.question_content:
Mateo went on a cross country trip with his grandfather. After they returned, they rounded the distance they had traveled to $3{,}200$ kilometers. 

**Which could be the number of kilometers Mateo and his grandfather traveled if the distance was rounded to the nearest hundred?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $3{,}200$ is $3{,}150$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $3{,}200$ is $3{,}249$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $3{,}150$ through $3{,}249$ rounds to $3{,}200$.
The distance Mateo and his grandfather traveled could have been  $3{,}238$ kilometers or $3{,}183$ kilometers.

assessment_items.question_content:
A pilot on an airplane rounds the altitude of the plane and tells the passengers they have reached a cruising altitude of $11{,}000$ meters. 

**Which could be the altitude of the plane if the altitude was rounded to the nearest thousand?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $11{,}000$ is $10{,}500$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $11{,}000$ is $11{,}499$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $10{,}500$ through $11{,}499$ rounds to $11{,}000$.
The altitude of the plane could be $11{,}400$ meters or $10{,}800$ meters.

assessment_items.question_content:
An ice cream shop has a sign in their window that says, "We sell $12{,}000$ scoops per month." The number of scoops has been rounded.

**Which could be the number of scoops sold if they rounded to the nearest thousand?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $12{,}000$ is $11{,}500$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $12{,}000$ is $12{,}499$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $11{,}500$ through $12{,}499$ rounds to $12{,}000$.
The number of scoops sold could be $12{,}111$ or $11{,}589$.

assessment_items.question_content:
In a brochure about an outdoor wilderness camp, the yearly attendance is rounded to $28{,}000$ students.

**Which could be the number of students who attended the camp if the attendance was rounded to the nearest thousand?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $28{,}000$ is $27{,}500$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $28{,}000$ is $28{,}499$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $27{,}500$ through $28{,}499$ rounds to $28{,}000$.
The attendance who attended the camp could be $27{,}500$ and $28{,}350$.

assessment_items.question_content:
An amusement park rounds its total attendance for last year to $120{,}000$ people.

**Which could have been the attendance at the amusement park if it was rounded to the nearest ten thousand?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $120{,}000$ is $115{,}000$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $120{,}000$ is $124{,}999$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $115{,}000$ through $124{,}999$ rounds to $120{,}000$.
The attendance at the amusement park could have been  $116{,}390$ people.

assessment_items.question_content:
A certain magazine rounds the number of people who subscribe to $800{,}000$.

**Which could be the number of people who subscribe to the magazine if it was rounded to the nearest hundred thousand?**

[[☃ radio 2]]
hints:
The smallest whole number that rounds **up** to $800{,}000$ is $750{,}000$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

The largest whole number that rounds **down** to $800{,}000$ is $849{,}999$.

[[☃ image 1]]
So, any whole number from $750{,}000$ through $849{,}999$ rounds to $800{,}000$.
The number of people who subscribe to the magazine could be $775{,}612$ and $849{,}212$.
